title: podman 5.x and pasta gateway crashes fedora40 completely when virtualbox 7.0.18 is running
body:
### Issue Description

rootless podman container and explictly specifying pasta as network crashes succesfully my fc40 system

Example command:
```
podman run --rm -it --network pasta:--map-gw  09db474e1757 bash
```

By pressing return, this effectively stops my system. I need to hard-reset the laptop (hold power-button for several seconds) to reboot.


Operating System: Fedora Linux 40
KDE Plasma Version: 6.0.5
KDE Frameworks Version: 6.2.0
Qt Version: 6.7.1
Kernel Version: 6.8.11-300.fc40.x86_64 (64-bit)
Graphics Platform: Wayland
Processors: 16 × AMD Ryzen 7 PRO 7840U w/ Radeon 780M Graphics
Memory: 58,6 GiB of RAM
Graphics Processor: AMD Radeon Graphics
Manufacturer: LENOVO
Product Name: 21K5000JGE
System Version: ThinkPad P14s Gen 4

Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.1.0
API Version:  5.1.0
Go Version:   go1.22.3
Built:        Wed May 29 02:00:00 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

comments.body:
it's even worse... just running podman in parallel to virtualbox is enough .... maybe pasta is still the culprit, because this is the new default for rootless-containers, but this is just an assumption.

podman should really check if vbox is running as a safeguard and maybe deny starting containers, because a system crash severly impacts the folder structure of podman.
i had to do a 'podman system reset' to recover

Well this is not a pasta or podman issue I would think. Pasta and podman runs entirely in user space so unless they trigger oom situation which should happen that fast I guess this is really something broken in the kernel if user space program can cause a crash.

Do you see a proper kernel panic and your host? I really don't know enough about virtual box but there is https://www.virtualbox.org/ticket/22072 already filled.

Is --map-gw actually required? Or does it also fail without it.
And to rule out podman you can just run something like `pasta --config-net true` directly from your cli to see if it reproduces the crash.

thanks for the issue in vbox:
There is additional info by Derek
``` A couple extra points of info. podman rootless container with slirp4netns doesn't have the same issue. So this is limited to podman with patsa (default in 5.0). ```


Your question:
--map-gw is not required (see 2nd. comment). Just run a container.

As vbox errors can take a while to fix, wouldn't it still be a good idea to check on podmans side (and refuse running with pasta)?

Journalctl does not even show a kernel-panic. The last useful information was this:
```
May 31 10:58:06 p14s NetworkManager[2832]: <info>  [1717145886.0314] device (vboxnet0): carrier: link connected
May 31 10:58:06 p14s kernel: vboxnet0: entered promiscuous mode
May 31 10:58:06 p14s kernel: vboxdrv: 000000009e1abcdb VBoxDDR0.r0
May 31 10:58:06 p14s kernel: VBoxNetFlt: attached to 'enp1s0f0' / 74:5d:22:f9:77:8a
May 31 10:58:06 p14s kernel: VBoxNetFlt: attached to 'eth0' / 38:7c:76:1b:40:0e
May 31 10:58:06 p14s audit: ANOM_PROMISCUOUS dev=enp1s0f0 prom=256 old_prom=0 auid=10001 uid=10001 gid=10001 ses=3
May 31 10:58:06 p14s kernel: r8169 0000:01:00.0 enp1s0f0: entered promiscuous mode
May 31 10:58:06 p14s kernel: r8152 8-1.4.4.3:1.0 eth0: entered promiscuous mode
May 31 10:58:06 p14s kernel: r8152 8-1.4.4.3:1.0 eth0: Promiscuous mode enabled
May 31 10:58:06 p14s audit: ANOM_PROMISCUOUS dev=eth0 prom=256 old_prom=0 auid=10001 uid=10001 gid=10001 ses=3
May 31 10:58:07 p14s avahi-daemon[2242]: Joining mDNS multicast group on interface vboxnet0.IPv6 with address fe80::800:27ff:fe00:0.
May 31 10:58:07 p14s avahi-daemon[2242]: New relevant interface vboxnet0.IPv6 for mDNS.
May 31 10:58:07 p14s avahi-daemon[2242]: Registering new address record for fe80::800:27ff:fe00:0 on vboxnet0.*.
```

Then... crash

> As vbox errors can take a while to fix, wouldn't it still be a good idea to check on podmans side (and refuse running with pasta)?

Well this is pretty much the most complicated part. How would we know which version triggers this under what circumstances? What if they fix the bug then podman still refuses to run unless we then update podman to no longer check this, etc... So many if's that it is just nor piratical.

So let's understand what is happening first. Assuming is has to do anything with the interface name please try with `--network pasta:-I,tap0` this will assign the same name as slirp4netns does.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

This is fixed in VirtualBox 7.0.20.  Reverting `default_rootless_network_cmd="slirp4netns"` in `containers.conf` goes back to 5.0's default pasta network command.  With a VirtualBox VM running and a rootless podman container with pasta there's no issues.

@slartibart70 thanks for reporting this, and apologies for the delay: coordinated disclosure of the original issue took a bit. This is CVE-2024-21161 and, as @derekschrock mentioned, is [fixed](https://www.oracle.com/security-alerts/cpujul2024.html#AppendixOVIR) in VirtualBox 7.0.20.

I believe we can close this now, let me know if it's fixed for you.

can confirm, works beautifully
no more crashes (no more fear of accidentally starting vbox while some container were running :-)
